outbound_official.note: no official Timor-Leste government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Cooperation (mnec.gov.tl) has a Services/Citizens section but publishes no consolidated list of visa-free/visa-on-arrival/e-visa destinations for Timorese ordinary-passport holders. The government portal (timor-leste.gov.tl), Immigration Service (migracao.gov.tl), and Customs Authority (customs.gov.tl) cover only inbound visa policy. The mnec.gov.tl/node/29 page (Europe relations) mentions the EU visa-waiver (2015) and UK visa exemption for Timorese citizens, but is not a structured outbound visa-list. Checked: mnec.gov.tl, mnec.gov.tl/citizen, mnec.gov.tl/node/29, timor-leste.gov.tl, migracao.gov.tl. No India-equivalent (mea.gov.in/vffin) origin-side page found.

visa_free.nationality: Schengen Area (EU member states fully applying Schengen acquis, plus Norway, Iceland, Switzerland, Liechtenstein)
visa_free.notes: Visa-free under EU-Timor-Leste Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement signed 26 May 2015 (provisional application from signing; ratified by European Parliament 15 December 2015). Maximum 90 days in any 180-day period. Covers all passport categories (ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, special). Excludes paid employment. UK and Ireland excluded (not part of Schengen area). Norway, Iceland, Switzerland, Liechtenstein covered by associated bilateral declarations on same terms.

Portuguese nationals receive visa exemption for tourism per Immigration Service of Timor-Leste (migracao.gov.tl) and Customs Authority (customs.gov.tl). Also covered by Schengen agreement above. Portuguese nationals (along with US and Indonesian nationals) are exempt from the advance Visa Application Authorization requirement at land border crossings. Duration not specified explicitly on official pages beyond 'visa exemption for tourism'.

US nationals are visa-exempt for tourism per Timor-Leste Customs Authority (customs.gov.tl). US nationals (along with Portuguese and Indonesian nationals) are exempt from the advance Visa Application Authorization requirement at land border crossings. Duration not specified explicitly on official page.

Visa-free at all border posts including all land border crossings, Dili International Airport, and Dili Sea Port. Indonesian nationals confirmed as exempt from advance Visa Application Authorization at land borders per customs.gov.tl and migracao.gov.tl. Introduction date (September 19, 2019) cited from aggregators; specific press release URL on timor-leste.gov.tl not independently confirmed. Stay extendable once for same period.

Visa exemption for ordinary passport holders under bilateral agreement signed 21 July 2014. Covers travel for tourism, visits, and business. Agreement announced on Government of Timor-Leste official site.

Visa waiver for ordinary passport holders for tourism, shopping, and educational activities (expanded from earlier diplomatic/service-only coverage). Agreement signed 20-21 June 2024. Approved by Timor-Leste National Parliament 2 October 2024 (61 votes in favor). Enters into force after ratification by both parliaments. As of June 2026, dual-ratification status not confirmed on official TL government page.

Visa waiver covering Diplomatic, Official/Service, and Ordinary Passport holders. Approved by Timor-Leste National Parliament 2 October 2024 (61 votes in favor). Corroborated by US-ASEAN Business Council (July 2024). Entry into force pending ratification by both parties; Singapore-side ratification not confirmed on official TL government domain.

Initial diplomatic/service passport agreement signed 8 October 2024 (per timor-leste.gov.tl). Expanded to ordinary passport holders; entered into force 15 August 2025 per Laotian Times (September 2025 article). Official TL government confirmation of ordinary-passport coverage signed at the Oct 2024 signing per timor-leste.gov.tl (?p=40083). 30 days visa-free for ordinary passport holders.

Visa exemption for all passport types (diplomatic, official, service, and ordinary). Council of Ministers approved for parliamentary ratification 29 May 2025 (timor-leste.gov.tl). National Parliament ratified 24 June 2025 (58 votes in favor, 0 against) per Tatoli. Duration of 30 days inferred from Wikipedia and regional norms; not explicitly stated on official TL government page. Effective date not specified.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival available at Dili International Airport and Dili Sea Port only. Fee: USD $30 cash (single entry). Transit visa: USD $20 for stays under 3 days. Extendable in-country: USD $35 for up to 30 additional days or USD $75 for 30-60 additional days (per customs.gov.tl; migracao.gov.tl states $40 for extension — discrepancy between official pages). Maximum cumulative stay 90 days. Travelers must show: sufficient funds (USD $100 per entry + USD $50 per day of stay), valid passport 6+ months with at least one blank page, return/onward ticket, accommodation proof. Land border arrivals (except Indonesians, Portuguese, and US nationals) must obtain advance Visa Application Authorization.

visa_policy.notes: Timor-Leste does not operate an e-visa system as of June 2026. Most visitors obtain a 30-day visa on arrival at Dili Airport or Sea Port for USD $30. EU/Schengen citizens enjoy 90-day visa-free stays under the 2015 EU bilateral agreement. US nationals, Portuguese nationals, and Indonesian nationals are visa-exempt (US and Portuguese confirmed for tourism; Indonesians visa-free at all border posts including land borders). Timor-Leste became the 11th ASEAN member on 26 October 2025. Recent bilateral visa-free agreements (Laos ordinary-passport effective 15 Aug 2025; Malaysia ratified June 2025) reflect ASEAN accession-related visa liberalisation.

conditional_access.conditions: Visa exemption for holders of diplomatic and service passports only. Council of Ministers of Timor-Leste authorised Ambassador to Philippines to sign the agreement (per timor-leste.gov.tl press release). Signing date, effective date, and duration not confirmed on any official TL government page. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered.

Visa exemption for single or multiple entry for holders of diplomatic and official passports. Agreement signed 16 December 2024 in Dhaka, witnessed by President Jose Ramos Horta. Signatories: Timor-Leste Foreign Minister Bendito dos Santos Freitas and Bangladesh Home Advisor Md Jahangir Alam Chowdhury. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered. Sourced from Tatoli (national news agency); no official .gov.tl domain confirmation found.

Visa waiver limited to Diplomatic and Official/Service Passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered (explicitly distinguished from Singapore and Thailand agreements in Tatoli article). Approved by Timor-Leste National Parliament 2 October 2024 (61 votes in favor). Entry into force pending ratification by both parties.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists in Timor-Leste. No official government source was found announcing any such program. The Immigration Service (migracao.gov.tl) offers standard residency permits under Migration Law 11/2017 but no investor citizenship track.

programs.name: Temporary Resident Permit
programs.notes: Valid 2 years, renewable. Eligibility: professional employment, foreign spouses (married 2-5 years to Timorese), family reunification, human trafficking victims, exceptional circumstances. Fee: USD $100 issuance and renewal. Temporary permit holders become eligible for Permanent Residence after 6+ years.

programs.name: Permanent Resident Permit
programs.notes: No expiration. Eligibility: 10+ consecutive years legal residency; OR dependent minor children of nationals; OR spouse of Timorese citizen (5+ years marriage); OR temporary permit holder for 6+ years. Requirements: no serious criminal convictions, demonstrated financial stability and housing, positive economic or social contribution. Fee: USD $100 issuance. Law: Migration Law 11/2017.

validation_notes: ADDED: United States (USA) to visa_free — customs.gov.tl explicitly lists US nationals as visa-exempt for tourism and exempt from land-border Visa Application Authorization. Was absent from previous record despite being in the official source.

MOVED: Laos (LAO) from conditional_access (diplomatic/service only) to visa_free — timor-leste.gov.tl (?p=40083) confirms the Oct 2024 agreement covered ordinary passports; Laotian Times (Sept 2025) confirms entry into force for ordinary passports on 15 August 2025.

ADDED: Malaysia (MYS) to visa_free — new entry. Council of Ministers approved 29 May 2025 (timor-leste.gov.tl) and National Parliament ratified 24 June 2025 (Tatoli) for all passport types including ordinary. Was absent from previous record.
BRUNEI (BRN): Added explicit max_stay_days: 30 inferred from Tatoli article context (same 30-day framework as Singapore/Thailand). Remains in conditional_access as ordinary passports are NOT covered.
REMOVED: Laos from conditional_access (was there as diplomatic/service passport only — superseded by ordinary-passport entry now in visa_free).
SINGAPORE (SGP): Moved source note — original entry had max_stay_days:30 which is correct. Remains in visa_free. Ratification pending note added.
VOA EXTENSION FEE: Noted discrepancy — customs.gov.tl lists $35/$75; migracao.gov.tl lists $40 for extension. Both official; discrepancy flagged in visa_on_arrival notes.
timor-leste.be removed from official_domains — the Belgian embassy site is not a TL government domain.
EU agreement note updated: 'every six months' corrected to standard '180-day period' language consistent with official source.
gaps: USA visa-free duration not specified on customs.gov.tl or migracao.gov.tl — recorded as null.
Portugal visa-free duration not specified on official pages — recorded as null.
Malaysia visa-free stay duration not specified in Council of Ministers announcement or parliamentary ratification article; 30 days inferred from regional ASEAN norms.
Malaysia agreement entry-into-force date not specified in official TL government sources.
Singapore agreement: Singapore-side parliamentary ratification date not confirmed on any official source; dual-ratification entry-into-force status unclear.
Thailand agreement: dual-ratification (both parliaments) status not confirmed on official TL government page as of June 2026.
No official TL government page enumerates a complete structured list of all visa-free/visa-on-arrival nationalities.
No official e-visa portal or system as of June 2026 (confirmed by absence on migracao.gov.tl).
No investor-specific residency or golden visa program found on any official page.
ASEAN free-movement provisions for Timor-Leste as new member (Oct 2025) not yet documented on official TL government pages.

Cambodia, Vietnam, Cuba, China, India: mentioned in Wikipedia as having diplomatic/service passport visa exemptions but no individual official TL government press release (timor-leste.gov.tl, customs.gov.tl, migracao.gov.tl) confirmed for each — omitted per official-source-first rule.
Bangladesh (BGD) diplomatic/official agreement: sourced from Tatoli (national news agency, not .gov domain); no official government ministry confirmation found.
Brunei (BRN) agreement: sourced from Tatoli; no official .gov.tl domain confirmation found.
Philippines agreement: Council of Ministers authorization confirmed on timor-leste.gov.tl, but signed agreement text, effective date, and duration not found.
VOA extension fee discrepancy: customs.gov.tl ($35/$75) vs migracao.gov.tl ($40) — both official sources, unresolved.
UN Passport and Interpol Passport holders: Wikipedia states 90-day visa-free entry but no official TL government source confirms this rule.
Indonesia visa-free introduction date (19 September 2019) not confirmed via official timor-leste.gov.tl press release.
